query: i ran specy:domain-extract-from-code on our billing service and the resulting specy/billing.domain is a mess — anemic entities, booleans everywhere. can you redesign it properly?
take the extracted loan.domain plus the refactoring.report and give me a clean DDD model
the distilled model has primitive obsession all over (raw decimals for money). refactor specy/orders.domain into proper value objects and aggregates
turn specy/inventory.domain (reverse-engineered from our legacy code) into a model a DDD purist would approve of, fixing the smells in refactoring.report
redesign the payments domain from first principles — the extracted one mirrors the database tables, not the business
we reverse-engineered a .domain from the monolith; now make it actually good DDD
clean up the god entity in specy/customer.domain and split it into a tight aggregate with proper boundaries
the refactoring report flags flag-driven state on Shipment. rework the extracted domain so the illegal states are unrepresentable
produce proper-loan.domain from the extracted loan.domain, fixing every smell and telling me which fix addresses which smell
our extracted domain model has generic 'OrderUpdated' events and no error paths — refactor it into intent-revealing domain events with explicit error events
design a domain model for a brand-new ride-sharing product from these product requirements and prose notes
extract a domain model from our Spring Boot codebase under ./src — we have no .domain yet
generate the Java/Spring code from specy/orders.domain
walk me through what the payment context actually does in specy/payment.domain, I just want to understand it
refactor this Python function to reduce nesting and pull out a helper
write EARS system requirements from our PRD for the checkout feature
my specy/loan.domain won't parse with the CLI — fix the syntax errors so it validates
rename all the OrderStatus enum values to camelCase across specy/orders.domain
create a PRD for a subscription billing product, capture personas and goals
review my React component folder structure for better separation of concerns
